# Experiments on hyper-k-mers {#sec-kfc-supplementary}

## Datasets {#sec-kfc-datasets}

@tbl-datasets lists the datasets used in the experiments and their characteristics.

:::{#tbl-datasets .large-table}
| Name | Type | Coverage | \# reads | Total length | Min. | Avg. | Max. |
|:--|:--:|--:|--:|---:|-:|--:|--:|
| `SRR11434954` | HiFi | 5000× | 1,789,131 | 23,122,913,014 | 46 | 12,924.1 | 26,294 |
| `SRR28370642` | ONT Duplex | 50× | 114,703 | 236,908,842 | 349 | 2,065.4 | 126,029 |
| `SRR28370651` | ONT Duplex | 50× | 109,061 | 226,502,819 | 330 | 2,076.8 | 125,012 |
| `SRR28370668` | ONT Simplex | 2000× | 6,819,683 | 9,101,103,830 | 1 | 1,334.5 | 396,011 |

E. Coli datasets used for benchmarking KFC. Min., Avg. and Max. refer to the minimum, average and maximum read length in bases.
:::

## Multi-threading efficiency {#sec-kfc-multithread}

KFC effectively utilizes multi-core architectures, achieving performance gains with up to several dozen cores before experiencing diminishing returns (@fig-plot-ont-100x-multithread).

:::{#fig-plot-ont-100x-multithread}
<!-- ![](../fig/hkmer/MTecolihifi) -->

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/MTecolihifi.png){width=80%}

Multi-core usage efficiency of KFC on the 100× HiFi E. coli dataset.
:::

## Effect of coverage {#sec-kfc-coverage}

@fig-combined-benchmark-coverage shows benchmarks similar to @fig-combined-benchmark-all but at various coverage levels, without significant change in the relative behaviors of the tools.

:::{#fig-combined-benchmark-coverage layout-ncol=2}
<!-- ![](../fig/hkmer/combined-benchmark-coverage) -->

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecolihifi50xmem)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecolihifi50xtime)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecoliont200xtime)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecoliont200xmem)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecoliont500xtime)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecoliont500xmem)

/kmer counting benchmark on the ONT Simplex E. coli dataset (SRR28370668) at 50×, 200× and 500× coverage.
Each subfigure shows memory usage (left) and runtime (right) as a function of /kmer size $k$.
:::

@fig-plot-gut-full shows the benchmark on the complete (unsampled) HiFi human gut dataset.

:::{#fig-plot-gut-full layout-ncol=2}
<!-- ![](../fig/hkmer/plot-gut-full) -->

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/gutfullmem)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/gutfulltime)

/kmer counting benchmark on the complete HiFi human gut datasets (SRR15275210, SRR15275211, SRR15275212, SRR15275213), filtering /kmers appearing once or twice (abundance threshold $t = 3$).
:::

## Effect of not filtering unique /kmers {#sec-kfc-nofilter}

@fig-combined-benchmark-nofilter shows the same benchmarks as @fig-combined-benchmark-all but without filtering unique /kmers (abundance threshold $t = 1$).
Jellyfish's performance is unchanged; FastK and KMC's performance degrades significantly in both time and memory.
KFC remains competitive even without filtering, compared to its competitors that do filter.

:::{#fig-combined-benchmark-nofilter layout-ncol=2}
<!-- ![](../fig/hkmer/combined-benchmark-nofilter) -->

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/duplexab1mem)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/duplexab1time)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecolihifi100xab1mem)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecolihifi100xab1time)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecoliont100xab1mem)

![](../fig/hkmer/plots/ecoliont100xab1time)

Comparison of /kmer counting benchmarks on ONT Duplex, PacBio HiFi and ONT Simplex E. coli datasets, without filtering unique /kmers.
:::

## KFC's algorithm {#sec-kfc-algorithm}

@alg-kfc gives an overview of the two passes of KFC.

::: {#alg-kfc}

```pseudocode
#| html-no-end: true
#| html-indent-lines: true
#| html-line-number: false
#| html-comment-delimiter: "//"
#| pdf-line-number: false
#| pdf-no-end: true
#| pdf-indent-lines: true
#| pdf-italic-comment: true
#| pdf-right-comment: true
#| pdf-comment-color: "darkgray"
#| pdf-comment-delimiter: "//"

\begin{algorithmic}
\Function{CountHkmer}{$read\_set \in (\Sigma^*)^*$; $k$, $m$, $t \in \mathbb{N}$ with $m \leq k$}
    \State $sk\_count \gets$ empty multimap
    \Comment{maps minimizer to $(hash(sk), count(sk))$}
    \State $hk\_count \gets$ empty multimap
    \Comment{maps minimizer to $(id\_left, id\_right, count(hk))$}
    \State $hk\_parts \gets$ empty vector of strings
    \Comment{2 bits per character}
    \State
    \Comment{First pass}
    \For{$read$ in $read\_set$}
    \Comment{can be parallelized}
        \For{$(prev\_sk, sk, next\_sk)$ in $read$}
            \State $m \gets$ minimizer of $sk$
            \If{$(hash(sk), c) \in sk\_count[m]$}
                \State $c \gets c + 1$; update $sk\_count$
            \Else
                \State $c \gets 1$; add $(hash(sk), 1)$ to $sk\_count[m]$
            \EndIf
            \If{$c = t$}
            \Comment{$sk$ just became solid: insert its /hkmer}
                \If{$IsSolid(sk\_count, prev\_sk, t)$}
                    \State $id\_left \gets$ id of right part of $prev\_sk$ in $hk\_count$
                \Else
                    \State $id\_left \gets |hk\_parts|$; append left part of $sk$ to $hk\_parts$
                \EndIf
                \If{$IsSolid(sk\_count, next\_sk, t)$}
                    \State $id\_right \gets$ id of left part of $next\_sk$ in $hk\_count$
                \Else
                    \State $id\_right \gets |hk\_parts|$; append right part of $sk$ to $hk\_parts$
                \EndIf
                \State add $(id\_left, id\_right, c)$ to $hk\_count[m]$
            \ElsIf{$c > t$}
            \Comment{$sk$ already indexed: update count}
                \State $(id\_left, id\_right, exact) \gets$ \Call{LookupAndUpdate}{$hk\_count, hk\_parts, sk$}
                \If{not $exact$}
                    \State add $(id\_left, id\_right, 1)$ to $hk\_count[m]$
                \EndIf
            \EndIf
        \EndFor
    \EndFor
    \State
    \Comment{Second pass: handle first/last super-k-mers and non-solid super-k-mers sharing a minimizer with a solid one}
    \For{$read$ in $read\_set$}
    \Comment{can be parallelized}
        \For{$sk$ in $read$}
            \State $m \gets$ minimizer of $sk$
            \If{$first\_sk$ or $last\_sk$}
                \State $(id\_left, id\_right, exact) \gets$ \Call{LookupAndUpdate}{$hk\_count, hk\_parts, sk$}
                \If{not $exact$}
                    \If{$id\_left = -1$}
                        \State $id\_left \gets |hk\_parts|$; append left part of $sk$ to $hk\_parts$
                    \EndIf
                    \If{$id\_right = -1$}
                        \State $id\_right \gets |hk\_parts|$; append right part of $sk$ to $hk\_parts$
                    \EndIf
                    \State add $(id\_left, id\_right, 1)$ to $hk\_count[m]$
                \EndIf
            \ElsIf{not $IsSolid(sk\_count, sk, t)$}
                \State \Call{LookupAndUpdate}{$hk\_count, hk\_parts, sk$}
            \EndIf
        \EndFor
    \EndFor
    \State \Return $sk\_count, hk\_count, hk\_parts$
\EndFunction

\Function{IsSolid}{$sk\_count$, $sk$, $t$}
\Comment{checks if a super-k-mer has reached threshold $t$}
    \For{$(h, c) \in sk\_count[minimizer(sk)]$}
        \If{$h = hash(sk)$}
            \State \Return $c \geq t$
        \EndIf
    \EndFor
    \State \Return $false$
\EndFunction

\Function{LookupAndUpdate}{$hk\_count$, $hk\_parts$, $sk$}
\Comment{Returns $(id\_left, id\_right, exact)$; increments count on exact match.}
    \State $id\_left, id\_right \gets -1, -1$
    \Comment{$-1$: sentinel for ``not found''}
    \For{$(l, r, c) \in hk\_count[minimizer(sk)]$}
        \If{$(hk\_parts[l], hk\_parts[r]) = sk$}
            \State $c \gets c + 1$; \Return $(l, r, true)$
        \EndIf
        \If{$sk$ starts with $hk\_parts[l]$} \State $id\_left \gets l$ \EndIf
        \If{$sk$ ends with $hk\_parts[r]$} \State $id\_right \gets r$ \EndIf
    \EndFor
    \State \Return $(id\_left, id\_right, false)$
\EndFunction
\end{algorithmic}
```

KFC's two-pass /kmer counting algorithm using /hkmers.
:::
